title: rubus victoris
intro: Rubus victoris, also known as the Victor's thimbleberry or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, known for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Rubus victoris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also consumed raw as a fruit, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects.
